policies.name: CII network configuration change control
policies.description: An agent proposing a configuration change on a system designated as critical information infrastructure must clear the operator's change-control board first. Designated CII owners in Singapore carry statutory duties to comply with codes of practice covering change management.
regulatory_references: Singapore Cybersecurity Act 2018 s.11 — Duty to comply with codes of practice
Australia SOCI Act 2018 Part 2A — Critical infrastructure risk management program

policies.name: Core routing / BGP change takeover
policies.description: Edits to BGP announcements or core-router policy can blackhole national traffic in seconds, so a senior network engineer must own the change end to end. Route leaks and hijacks propagate globally before any rollback can land.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — MANRS routing security actions for network operators

policies.name: Mass outage customer notification sign-off
policies.description: A drafted outage notification going to the whole customer base must be approved before send: wording drives regulator scrutiny and triple-zero/995 welfare checks. Australia now prescribes outage communications content and timing by industry standard.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications (Customer Communications for Network Outages) Industry Standard 2024
IMDA Telecommunication Service Resilience Code of Practice

policies.name: Interconnect and peering arrangement changes
policies.description: Modifying interconnect agreements or IX peering sessions affects competing carriers' traffic and is governed by the dominant-licensee obligations in the Singapore competition code. Commercial and regulatory review happens before the agent files anything.
regulatory_references: Singapore Code of Practice for Competition in the Provision of Telecommunication Services (Telecom Competition Code) — interconnection obligations

policies.name: Planned maintenance window notice
policies.description: Issuing a routine planned-maintenance notice for a pre-approved change window is safe to automate; the window itself was already vetted through change control.

policies.name: Network health and KPI reporting
policies.description: Read-only queries against network health dashboards and service KPI reports carry no actuation risk and run fully automated.

policies.name: CII cybersecurity incident notification
policies.description: Statutory incident notifications to CSA or the Australian Cyber and Infrastructure Security Centre run on tight clocks (hours, not days) and bind the licensee's legal position — a designated incident manager must author and lodge the report.
regulatory_references: Singapore Cybersecurity Act 2018 s.14 — Duty to report cybersecurity incidents
Australia SOCI Act 2018 s.30BC — Mandatory reporting of critical cyber security incidents

policies.name: Lawful interception request — legal channel only
policies.description: Any request to activate interception of communications goes exclusively to the carrier's lawful-intercept legal unit, which verifies the warrant or statutory authorisation before anything is provisioned. No automated system may acknowledge, action, or even confirm the existence of an intercept.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979 Chapter 2 — Interception warrants
India Telecommunications Act 2023 s.20 — Interception and suspension powers

policies.name: Law-enforcement customer data disclosure
policies.description: Police or agency requests for subscriber records must be matched against a valid production order or statutory authorisation by the disclosure team before a single record leaves the carrier. Australian carriers' duty to assist agencies sits alongside strict limits on what may be handed over.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications Act 1997 s.313 — Assistance to agencies
Australia Telecommunications Act 1997 Part 13 — Protection of communications information

policies.name: Cell-site and subscriber location data access
policies.description: Cell-site records and live location traces reveal a person's movements and may only be released under a Chapter 4 authorisation or equivalent instrument, verified by a human. Agents must never resolve a location query directly, even for apparently internal requesters.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979 Chapter 4 — Access to telecommunications data

policies.name: Interception capability configuration change
policies.description: Structured agent calls that would alter mediation-platform or interception-capability configuration require sign-off from the LI compliance owner: capability plans are filed with the regulator and drift breaches the licence.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979 Part 5-4 — Interception capability plans

policies.name: Retained telecommunications metadata query
policies.description: Queries against the mandatory data-retention store (call records, IP allocations held under Part 5-1A) need approval from the records custodian, who confirms the requester's authority and logs the access for audit.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications (Interception and Access) Act 1979 Part 5-1A — Data retention

policies.name: OT/SCADA write command from IT systems
policies.description: Write commands originating in the IT estate — setpoint changes, RTU dispatch, PLC downloads — are blocked at the IT/OT boundary outright. Actuation of plant happens only from the engineering workstation inside the OT zone, per zone-and-conduit segmentation.
regulatory_references: Australia SOCI Act 2018 Part 2A — Critical infrastructure risk management program
Industry best practice — ISA/IEC 62443 zone and conduit segmentation

policies.name: Industrial safety-system override interdiction
policies.description: Requests to bypass a safety interlock, disable an emergency shutdown, or force a safety instrumented system are never automatable under any configuration — defeating the last line of protection is how people die. This interdiction cannot be tuned or disabled by any tenant.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — IEC 61511 safety instrumented systems lifecycle

policies.name: Dam, grid and water telemetry threshold override
policies.description: Raising or suppressing alarm thresholds on dam gates, grid frequency, or water-treatment dosing telemetry masks developing failures across an entire asset class, so a control-room engineer must take the action personally with a second person verifying.
regulatory_references: Australia SOCI Act 2018 Part 2A — Critical infrastructure risk management program
Singapore Cybersecurity Act 2018 s.11 — Duty to comply with codes of practice

policies.name: OT historian read-only query
policies.description: Pulling read-only historian data (trends, alarm history) across the IT/OT boundary is permitted but flagged: repeated wide pulls can map plant behaviour for an attacker, so the access pattern feeds the risk score.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — ISA/IEC 62443-3-3 SR 5.1 network segmentation

policies.name: Vendor remote access into the OT network
policies.description: Granting a vendor a remote session into the SCADA or OT network is a standing CCoP control point: the OT security lead approves the window, the jump-host path, and the named individual before the session is opened.
regulatory_references: CSA Cybersecurity Code of Practice for Critical Information Infrastructure (CCoP 2.0) — remote access controls

policies.name: Classified or restricted government data in prompts
policies.description: Material marked at SECRET, CONFIDENTIAL, or RESTRICTED under government classification schemes must never enter a commercial AI pipeline; processing it outside accredited systems is a wrongful-communication offence, not a policy preference. The block cannot be disabled by any tenant.
regulatory_references: Singapore Official Secrets Act 1935 s.5 — Wrongful communication of information
Singapore Government Instruction Manual 8 (IM8) — ICT security and data classification (comparator)

policies.name: Citizen identity data in government chat channels
policies.description: When a government chatbot conversation surfaces a national ID or passport number, the response is held for an officer's approval: confirming or echoing identity attributes in an unauthenticated chat is the classic vector for impersonated benefit claims.
regulatory_references: Singapore Public Sector (Governance) Act 2018 — data safeguards for public agencies
India DPDP Act 2023 s.8 — Data Fiduciary Obligations

policies.name: Cross-agency data sharing without an agreement
policies.description: Moving citizen datasets between agencies requires an executed data-sharing direction or agreement; an agent asked to transfer records inter-agency hands the request to the data governance office to confirm one exists and covers the fields requested.
regulatory_references: Singapore Public Sector (Governance) Act 2018 — data sharing directions
Singapore Government Instruction Manual 8 (IM8) — data management policies (comparator)

message_contains_any: share the dataset with another agency

policies.name: National ID system queries at scale
policies.description: Batch lookups against national identity systems — bulk NRIC matching, Aadhaar batch verification — are blocked: scale converts a lawful single-record check into de facto identity-database exfiltration, which the Aadhaar Act expressly prohibits.
regulatory_references: India Aadhaar Act 2016 s.29 — Restriction on sharing identity information
Singapore PDPC Advisory Guidelines on the PDPA for NRIC and other National Identification Numbers (2018)

policies.name: Public-sector procurement communications
policies.description: Outbound messages touching live tenders — clarifications, evaluation notes, award notices — go through the procurement officer before release, because uneven disclosure to bidders can void the tender and expose the agency to challenge.
regulatory_references: Singapore Government Procurement Act 1997 — fair and open procurement obligations

policies.name: Routine citizen service information
policies.description: Answering published-information questions — office hours, application steps, fee schedules — in a government service channel involves no personal data and is the intended automation surface for citizen chatbots.

policies.name: Emergency-services call routing change
policies.description: Any change touching how 000, 995, or 112 calls route to answering points is owned end-to-end by a named emergency-services engineer — a misroute is a life-safety event and a breach of the carrier's emergency call determination. Never tunable by a tenant.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications (Emergency Call Service) Determination 2019
Singapore Telecommunications Act 1999 — licensee obligations for essential services

policies.name: Public emergency alert broadcast
policies.description: Initiating a cell-broadcast public warning reaches every handset in the target area and cannot be recalled; a false alert erodes the channel for real disasters. Only the authorised duty officer may trigger or cancel one.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — Common Alerting Protocol (CAP) governance for public warning systems

policies.name: Service restoration prioritisation review
policies.description: An agent-drafted restoration sequence after a major outage is reviewed for fairness before execution: hospitals, emergency services, and priority-assistance customers must rank ahead of commercially attractive accounts, and the rationale must survive regulator scrutiny.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — priority assistance and equitable restoration sequencing

policies.name: Emergency network traffic management
policies.description: Throttling or load-shedding plans during a disaster trade one user group's connectivity against another's; the network duty manager approves the plan and confirms emergency-services traffic is exempted before it is applied.
regulatory_references: IMDA Telecommunication Service Resilience Code of Practice

policies.name: SIM swap and number porting verification
policies.description: SIM replacement, eSIM transfer, and port-out requests are the front door for account-takeover fraud against banking OTPs, so a trained agent re-verifies the subscriber through an out-of-band channel before any swap executes.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — SIM-swap fraud controls aligned with IMDA and DoT advisories

message_contains_any: transfer my number to a new sim

policies.name: Subscriber usage and billing record disclosure
policies.description: Call detail records and itemised billing histories are protected communications information; a privacy officer checks the legal basis and the recipient before any export, because Part 13 makes unauthorised disclosure an offence for the carrier's staff and systems alike.
regulatory_references: Australia Telecommunications Act 1997 Part 13 — Protection of communications information
Singapore PDPA s.24 — Protection Obligation

policies.name: Subscriber self-service password reset
policies.description: Routine password resets on the subscriber self-care portal follow the standard verified flow and are the canonical low-risk automation case for a telco support agent.

policies.name: Bulk SMS campaign approval
policies.description: Launching a bulk SMS or sender-ID campaign needs the messaging compliance team to verify consent records, sender-ID registration, and unsubscribe handling — anti-spam statutes in both Singapore and Australia attach per-message penalties.
regulatory_references: Singapore Spam Control Act 2007 — unsolicited commercial electronic messages
Australia Spam Act 2003 s.16 — consent requirement
India TRAI Telecom Commercial Communications Customer Preference Regulations 2018

policies.name: VIP escalation on government accounts
policies.description: Escalations flagged as VIP on government or ministerial accounts get a human approver: pretexting as a senior official is a recurring social-engineering pattern against carrier support desks.

policies.name: Telecom billing dispute risk flag
policies.description: Billing disputes on telecom accounts stay automated but carry a risk flag, since disputed roaming and premium-rate charges feed both TIO-style complaints and chargeback fraud patterns worth tracking in aggregate.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — telecommunications industry ombudsman complaint-handling benchmarks

policies.name: Spectrum and licence filing review
policies.description: Draft spectrum applications and licence filings bind the carrier to build-out and fee obligations for years; regulatory affairs reviews the lot before anything is lodged with IMDA, ACMA, or DoT.
regulatory_references: India Telecommunications Act 2023 s.4 — Assignment of spectrum
IMDA Spectrum Management Handbook

policies.name: Licence condition breach response
policies.description: A show-cause notice or alleged breach of licence conditions threatens the authorisation the whole business runs on — responses are drafted under privilege by regulatory counsel, never sent by an automated agent.
regulatory_references: India Telecommunications Act 2023 — terms and conditions of authorisation
Singapore Telecommunications Act 1999 s.8 — licensing of telecommunication systems and services

policies.name: Large licence and spectrum fee payment
policies.description: Licence-fee and spectrum-charge payments above the operational threshold get finance sign-off before submission: misstated adjusted-gross-revenue calculations have produced decade-long disputes in the Indian market.
regulatory_references: Industry best practice — licence fee and adjusted gross revenue reconciliation controls

policies.name: Cross-border transfer of localised telecom data
policies.description: Proposals to mirror or migrate subscriber datasets offshore from Indonesia or Vietnam collide with hard localisation mandates; the data residency officer maps each dataset against the storage-in-country rules before any transfer is engineered.
regulatory_references: Indonesia Government Regulation 71/2019 art. 21 — electronic system data management
Vietnam Decree 53/2022/ND-CP art. 26 — data localisation requirements
